author: Bill Whalen - Microsoft
platform: Power Platform (Canvas App + Dataverse + Power Automate)

personas.demo_ee: Sonali Horan — RC Materials Planning Manager at Carrier

elevator_pitch: Carrier's aftermarket warehouse gets 190 inbound trucks a week but has dock space for only 20 a day. One person spends her entire Monday manually matching truck manifests against backlog spreadsheets to figure out which trucks to let in first. This app does that automatically — scores every truck by how many customer backorders it clears and how much revenue it unlocks, fills the schedule, and sends it to the warehouse in one click.

business_value.time_saved: ~6 hours per week (Monday spreadsheet process eliminated)
business_value.revenue_impact: Faster backlog clearance — $2.4M/week prioritized intelligently vs. guesswork
business_value.otif_improvement: Directly improves On-Time-In-Full metric (Carrier's #1 KPI direction)
business_value.sustainability: One person was doing this alone — not sustainable. Now automated.
business_value.future_phases: Phase 2 adds AI to read reschedule emails from TSPs + normalize 40 supplier formats

key_terms.OTIF: On Time In Full — Carrier's primary supply chain metric. Did the right parts arrive before the customer's order promise date?
key_terms.Backlog: Customer sales orders with no inventory to fill them. Trucks carrying backlog parts get priority.
key_terms.TSP: Transportation Service Provider — the trucking companies moving the trailers
key_terms.Kenco: Third-party logistics company operating Carrier's Chattanooga warehouse (the 'Kenco IB Scheduler' receives the dock appointment schedule)
key_terms.Detention/Demurrage: Fees Carrier pays when a trailer sits too long without being unloaded — a cost driver for Phase 2
key_terms.E2Open: External scheduling/logistics platform Carrier uses — future integration target
key_terms.TMS ID: Transport Management System ID — the unique identifier for each trailer

setup_checklist: Open Power Apps: https://make.powerapps.com → Environment: Mfg Gold Template
Navigate to Solutions → CarrierTrailerPrioritization → Canvas App
Have the demo JSON loaded in the RAPP demo player (demos.html) as a backup
Know your talking points: OTIF, revenue impact, 190 trailers, 6-hour manual process

steps.title: Set the Scene
steps.you_say: Sonali, I want to show you what we built based on our discovery conversation. You told us your team spends every Monday doing this manually in spreadsheets — matching 190 truck manifests against your backlog report. What I'm about to show you eliminates that entirely.
steps.what_to_show: The Canvas App Trailer Dashboard — KPI cards at the top
talking_points: Point to the KPI cards: '190 trailers scored, $2.4M in potential revenue clearance'
That number — $2.4M — is the revenue impact of all the backlog orders sitting in those trucks waiting to get in
Previously this was in a spreadsheet. Now it refreshes every Monday automatically from SAP
steps.demo_prompt: Show me this week's trailer schedule.
steps.expected_response: Full ranked table appears with 190 trailers, OTIF scores, revenue, dock assignments

steps.title: Explain the Scoring Logic
steps.you_say: Every truck gets a composite score. Let me show you why trailer TMS-4821 is #1 this week.
steps.what_to_show: Click on Trailer #1 or ask the agent to score it
talking_points: OTIF Score: 47 part numbers in this truck are on customer backorder right now
Revenue Impact: $128,000 of backlog clears the moment this truck unloads
We weight OTIF at 50%, revenue at 40% — your team can adjust those weights anytime in the Scoring Config
In Phase 2 we add detention cost — the fee you're paying Carrier for trailers sitting in the yard
steps.expected_response: Detailed scoring breakdown: 47 OTIF parts, $128K revenue, composite score 88.2/100

objection_handling.How does it know what's on backorder?: It pulls from your Power BI dataset — the same backlog report you look at manually today. Kevin's team identified the backend source.
objection_handling.What about detention costs?: Great question — that's Phase 2. Sonali mentioned that data comes by email from logistics. In Phase 2 we read that automatically and factor it into the score.
objection_handling.Can we adjust the weights?: Yes — there's a Scoring Config table. Change OTIF vs revenue weighting anytime without touching code.

steps.title: Show the Schedule Builder
steps.you_say: Now here's where the magic happens — the Schedule Builder. This is what replaces the spreadsheet.
talking_points: Left side: the unscheduled queue, ranked by priority
Right side: what's already scheduled this week
Top cards: dock capacity — Dock 1 (5/day), Dock 2 (15/day), Overflow (3/day)
The Auto-Schedule button assigns all 190 trailers in priority order in one click
Manual override is always available — drag any trailer to a different dock or date
steps.demo_prompt: Go ahead and assign it to Dock 2.
steps.expected_response: Trailer TMS-4821 assigned to Dock 2, Monday 08:00-10:00, notification queued
steps.expected_response_2: 190 trailers auto-assigned across 3 weeks by priority rank

steps.title: Send to Kenco
steps.you_say: Last step — the app drafts the email to your Kenco inbound scheduler automatically. One button.
talking_points: The email pre-populates with every confirmed trailer, dock, date, and time slot
Sonali reviews it, hits Send — Kenco gets it instantly
Previously Sonali would manually type this out and email it herself
CC list is pre-configured: Sonali, Kevin, any other stakeholders
steps.expected_response: Email confirmed sent, 115 trailers, $2.4M revenue, 6 hours saved vs manual

steps.title: Handle a Reschedule (Bonus)
steps.you_say: One more thing Sonali mentioned — TSPs sometimes call or email to reschedule mid-week. Here's how that works.
steps.what_to_show: Reschedule log at bottom of Communications screen
talking_points: Currently: TSP emails the inbound scheduler, she manually updates the spreadsheet
Phase 2 vision: AI reads the email automatically, extracts the trailer ID + new date, updates the schedule
Today: the log captures it in one place, notifies Sonali, slot gets reassigned to next priority trailer
steps.demo_prompt: Log a reschedule for TMS-3390 — driver mechanical issue, available Tuesday instead.
steps.expected_response: TMS-3390 status changed to Rescheduled, Sonali notified, TMS-5512 offered as replacement for that slot

steps.title: Wrap Up — Phase Roadmap
steps.you_say: So this is Phase 1 — live in your Mfg Gold Template environment today. Here's what Phase 2 and 3 look like.
steps.what_to_show: Nothing — this is a verbal close
talking_points: Phase 1 (done): SAP + SharePoint ingestion, OTIF + revenue scoring, Canvas App, schedule → Kenco
Phase 2: Add detention cost data, E2Open integration, AI reading reschedule emails
Phase 3: AI Builder normalizes the 40 different external supplier packing list formats automatically
Portable solution file — can be imported into your production environment when ready

common_questions.question: Is this live today?
common_questions.answer: Yes — the Dataverse tables, solution, and reference data are live in your Mfg Gold Template environment right now. The Canvas App screens are YAML-ready to paste into Power Apps Studio. We can have a working app you can click through in under an hour.

common_questions.question: Who built this and how long did it take?
common_questions.answer: Microsoft built this as a rapid prototype using the RAPP (Rapid AI Agent Production Pipeline) framework. The Dataverse schema, Canvas App, Power Automate flow specs, and Code App scaffold were built in a single session.

common_questions.question: What does Samyak / Lucas need to do to maintain this?
common_questions.answer: Very little — Power Automate handles the weekly ingestion automatically. The Canvas App is no-code. They can adjust scoring weights in the Scoring Config table without touching code. The field mapping table handles new supplier formats without code changes.

common_questions.question: How does it connect to SAP?
common_questions.answer: Power Automate has a native SAP connector. Samyak will need to configure the SAP connection credentials. For the non-SAP factories (Colleyville, Indy), it reads from SharePoint Excel automatically.

common_questions.question: What about the external suppliers with different formats?
common_questions.answer: Phase 1 handles the structured ones. The Field Mapping table in Dataverse acts as a synonym dictionary — map 'Truck ID' to 'TrailerID' once, and the flow normalizes it forever. Phase 3 adds AI Builder to handle the truly unstructured ones.

common_questions.question: Can this be exported to production?
common_questions.answer: Yes — we exported a CarrierTrailerPrioritization.zip solution file. Import it to any Power Platform environment with pac solution import. Zero rework.
